verdict_reasoning: IZUMO4 is a nuclear-resident protein belonging to the IZUMO family. NCBI localization data explicitly place it in the nucleus with no documented membrane topology, GPI anchor, or surface display in any cell state. The IZUMO family are canonically germline/gamete-restricted proteins involved in fertilization; even germline members with known surface roles (e.g., IZUMO1 on sperm flagellum) have explicit membrane anchors documented in the literature. IZUMO4's nuclear localization and absence of any TM domain annotation or surface biology in canonical sources support a strictly intracellular verdict.
